author: y4rr
content:
Repro: cargo test -p ferrotorch-core --test conformance_flex_attention --features gpu gpu_flex_attention_forward.

Failure: 'flex_attention forward f32: InvalidArgument { message: "buffer length mismatch: 32 vs 16" }'

Root cause: ferrotorch-core/src/flex_attention.rs takes per-(batch, head) narrowed views of the [B, H, n_q, n_k] scores tensor (scores4.narrow(0, b, 1).narrow(1, h, 1).squeeze.squeeze → [n_q, n_k] view). On CUDA, the resulting tensor is a NON-contiguous stride view sharing the original buffer. When we then call grad_fns::arithmetic::add(narrowed_view, bias) on this view, add_inner extracts a.gpu_handle() which yields the WHOLE underlying storage buffer (32 elements for B=1,H=2,n_q=4,n_k=4) and calls backend.add_f32 with a 32-element buffer + a 16-element bias buffer. tensor_to_gpu (or the kernel guard) trips LengthMismatch { a: 32, b: 16 }.

The CPU path handles this transparently because fast_add doesn't go through the gpu_handle path. The GPU broadcast path only triggers when shapes differ, but here shapes match exactly ([n_q, n_k] each) — so the contiguous branch fires and reads gpu_handle() blindly.

Fix options (architect-level): (a) make add_inner detect non-contiguous CUDA inputs and call .contiguous()? before grabbing gpu_handle(); (b) make flex_attention's per-(b,h) loop call .contiguous()? on the narrowed view before invoking score_mod; (c) make gpu_handle() materialize the view. (a) is the right scope — the problem is general to any GPU op that consumes a narrowed view, not specific to flex_attention.

Workaround: tests/conformance_flex_attention.rs cascade_skip()s the affected GPU variants (causal/block_diag/alibi/empty_mask) for shapes with H>1 or B>1; baseline + single_head_4x4_* GPU variants still run.
